content_body:
1. Nate Currin Performance

The Martyn House in Towne - April 4th, 2015

Love live music? Then check out Nate Currin's show on April 4th at 7:00 PM. An artist and a storyteller, Nate has traveled to over 20 countries and performed nearly 500 headlining shows throughout the U.S. Come see why he's been featured on MTV and toured with big names like Blues Traveler and Jars of Clay. For $15.00, you can see him perform and enjoy a small plate menu. Call 706-635-5565 or click here for more information.

 

2. Easter Egg Hunts

St. Episcopal Church - April 4th, 2015 (@ 1:00 PM)

OR 

West Tennessee Ave Ball Field - April 4th, 2015 (@ 2:00 PM)

Take your pick of two great Easter Egg Hunt options in the area! At 1:00 PM on April 4th, the Annual Blue Ridge Community Easter Egg Hunt will be hosted at St. Luke's Episcopal Church. This event is free and open to anyone, and will include both real and plastic eggs, as well as refreshments. There will also be a dedicated area for very young children. Click here for more information.

Or, for children under 12, the Annual Copper Basin Easter Egg Hunt will be held on the same day at 2:00 PM. There wil be 1,000 hidden eggs around the ball field, including prize eggs and a grand prize egg for each age group. There will also be free photos with the Easter Bunny! Click here for more details.

 

3. Acapalooza Music Performance

Glenn Auditorium at Young Harris College - April 9th, 2015

Come enjoy a cappella pop song arrangements brought to you by the Young Harris College Music Department. The Chamber Choir will team up with Compulsive Lyres and Southern Harmony for a memorable performance you won't want to miss. Call 706-379-5182 for more details! 

 

4. The Moving of Lilla Barton

Blue Ridge Community Theater - April 9th - 26th, 2015

A dramatic comedy, The Moving of Lilla Barton, is the tale of a small town's dilemma over how to get the widowed Lilla Barton to move out of the parsonage after her Reverend husband's passing. With a pushy realtor eager to get her out, a Southern gentleman who believes she should be alowed to stay, and a Bishop who wanted to marry Lilla in the past, there is sure to be plenty of drama and laughter. The show runs from April 9th to April 26th and starts at 7:30 PM Thursday through Saturday. Sundays the show starts at 2:00 PM. Call 706-632-9223 or visit the Blue Ridge Community Theater's website for more details.

 

5. Breathe Deep Blue Ridge

Mercier Orchards - April 11th, 2015

Come to Mercier Orchards for the 3rd Annual Breathe Deep Blue Ridge and help raise awareness about lung cancer. Started and managed by the Mercier Family Orchards after a family member was diagnosed with stage IV lung cancer—despite the fact that neither she nor any of her family members were ever smokers. This 5K walk/run will take you through the apple blossoms at Merceir Orchards and help raise money for the LUNGeveity Foundation. The route is pet-friendly and there will be food and live entertainment. Call 706-632-3411 or click here for details!

 

6. Kiwanis Spring Fair

Kiwanis Fairgrounds - April 14th - 18th, 2015

Come to the Kiwanis Fairgrounds to enjoy rides, fairway, and carnival goodies! The fair will be open from 5:00 PM to 11:00 PM from Tuesday, April 14th, through Friday, April 17th, and from 1:00 PM to 11:00 PM on Saturday, April 18th. For more information 706-258-3247 or 706-455-0998.

 

7. Cosmic Concert: Pink Floyd's The Wall

Rollins Planetarium at Young Harris College - April 17th & April 24th, 2015

Pink Floyd's The Wall is considered to be one of the best rock albums of all time. Now, you can experience the powerful music along with mesmerizing laser art under the dome of Rollins Planetarium. The show will be at 7:00 PM and 8:00 PM on Friday, April 17th, and Friday, April 24th. Call 706-379-5195 or click here for more information.

 

8. Taste of Blue Ridge

Willow Creek Falls - April 18th, 2015


Sponsored by Humane Society of Blue Ridge, the 8th Annual Taste of Blue Ridge event will features food from local restaurants, drinks, live music, and both silent and live auctions. Come eat, dance, and bid on April 18th at 5:00 PM. For more information call (706) 632-5224 or visit Taste of Blue Ridge's Facebook page.

 

9. Bear on the Square Mountain Music & Folk Art Festival

Downtown Dahlonega Square - April 18th - 19th, 2015

Get ready for an exciting weekend of music, art, and award-winning storytelling! The 19th annual Bear on the Square Mountain Festival will be held the third weekend in April, and you won't want to miss it! Come see why the National Storytelling Network has named this event the 2015 Southeast Regional Spotlight Event for Storytelling. Click here for more information.

 

10. Classic Cars Cruise In

Dairy Queen Parking Lot - April 24th, 2015

The name says it all. Show off your classic car on April 24th or just come to enjoy the show. It begins at 6:00 pm and goes on until dark. Find out more by calling (706) 838 - 4701.

 

11. Spring Fest Arts & Crafts Sale

State Farmers Market in Blue Ridge - April 25th - 26th, 2015

Come check out this free event where you'll find over 100 arts and crafts vendors selling wood carvings, pottery, and quilts. There will be homemade snacks, canned goods, and unique trinkets for sale, as well. Combine that with live music and lots of food, and you have the perfect weekend of fun. This even will be open from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Saturday, April 25th, and Sunday, April 26th. Call 423-761-9985 for more information.
title: 11 Awesome April Activities in Blue Ridge
